title: Suspension Weave
objective: Carry a chain of at least three suspensions in a florid line above the given descending cantus: the disposition exercise - dissonance placed ON the strong beat, prepared and prepaid.
prompt: Replace rests in V0 with a parser-valid florid line whose backbone is a suspension chain: notes attacked on half-beat rows with duration at least 1, sustaining across the following downbeat ('-' on the integer row), dissonant against the new bass tone on that downbeat, resolving DOWN one step at the next half-beat. At least three such suspensions must be genuine dissonances (seconds, fourths, sevenths, or tritones against the bass). Each resolution may serve as the next preparation. Decorate the remaining half-beat positions with duration-0.5 cell tones (passing, neighbor, enclosure, escape) whose strong-beat boundaries stay consonant. Close with a final note of duration 1 at beat 7.
success_criteria: Every row remains strict parser-valid MidGrid.
V1 cantus remains unchanged, including hold cells.
At least three suspensions: attacked on a half-beat, sustained across the downbeat, dissonant there, resolved down one step at the next half-beat.
Added cell tones keep strong-beat boundaries consonant.
Corrected solution has no evaluator errors.
